session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9378 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Classify PR review feedback by filling in semantic fields from mechanical CLI output. 16→ 17→## Steps 18→ 19→1. **Run mechanical classification:** 20→ 21→ ```bash 22→ erk exec classify-pr-feedback [--pr <number>] [--include-resolved] 23→ ``` 24→ 25→ Pass `--pr <number>` if specified in `$ARGUMENTS`. Pass `--include-resolved` if specified in `$ARGUMENTS`. 26→ 27→ This returns JSON with mechanically pre-classified items: 28→ - **review_submissions**: PR-level reviews (classification: "actionable" | "informational" | "needs_llm") 29→ - **review_threads**: Inline review threads (pre_existing_candidate flag set for bot + restructured) 30→ - **discussion_comments**: Discussion comments (classification: "informational" | "needs_llm")
... [169 lines omitted] ...
104→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 106→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 144→ "error": null 159→## Error Case 173→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 191→ "error": null

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run mechanical PR feedback classification for PR 9378

session.assistant: Let me get the audit-pr-docs full details:
session.tool_result:
No violations detected. All code changes follow erk conventions.
<details> <summary>Details</summary>
### Tripwires Triggered
**CLI Category:** - Command deletion patterns (removal of create-from command) - Parameter handling and validation - Help text formatting
**Testing Category:** - Test file renaming and organization - Test assertions for new behavior
**Documentation Category:** - Documentation updates for command changes - Index file synchronization
### Tier 1 Pattern Matches ✅ `@patch|monkeypatch\.` - None found ✅ `Path\.home\(\)` - None found (no production code changes affecting this pattern)
### Tier 2 Semantic Matches ✅ **Removing a command without checking docs/learned/ for references** - Documentation properly updated (index.md, comparison doc updated, create-from.md deleted) ✅ **Removing a workflow command or CLI entry** - Command removed from __init__.py, no workflow references existed ✅ **Renaming CLI commands without checking workflow files** - No workflow file dependencies found ✅ **Command deletion without checking integration tests** - No gateway methods deleted, only command consolidation ✅ **Test assertions matching old behavior** - Tests properly updated for new --from-branch behavior
... [69 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me analyze and create the classified feedback JSON. Based on the mechanical output and the comments I've reviewed, I'll classify each item:
1. **Comment ID 4103665513** (schrockn): "Plan Queued for Implementation" - This is a metadata/workflow comment, informational in nature 2. **Comment ID 4103690029** (github-actions bot): "Dignified Python Review" - All checks passed, this is informational 3. **Comment ID 4103690134** (github-actions bot): "Dignified Code Simplifier Review" - All checks passed, this is informational 4. **Comment ID 4103690147** (github-actions bot): "Test Coverage Review" - All checks passed, this is informational 5. **Comment ID 4103696136** (github-actions bot): "Audit PR Docs" - Found 2 violations in `docs/learned/erk/same-worktree-navigation.md`, this needs action
